'''Samisoni Langi''' (born 11 June 1993) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|ce}} and for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]].

Langi previously played for the [[Sydney Roosters]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]], and for the [[Leigh Centurions]] and the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]], signing from the [[Penrith Panthers]] in June 2017. He has played for both [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] and [[France national rugby league team|France]] at international level. He played as a {{rlp|so}} earlier in his career.

==Early life==
Langi was born in [[Auburn, New South Wales]], [[Australia]] and is of [[Tongans|Tongan]] descent.

He played his junior rugby league for the [[Berala Bears]].<ref name=panthers /> While attending [[Trinity Catholic College, Auburn]], he was selected to play for the NSW Combined Catholic Colleges Schoolboys and the [[Australian Schoolboys rugby league team|Australian Schoolboys]] teams, both in 2011.

==Playing career==
===Sydney Roosters===
Langi played for [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] in the [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|National Youth Competition]] before being signed by the [[Sydney Roosters]]. On 20 April 2013, Langi made his international debut for Tonga in their Pacific International against [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]], scoring two tries and kicking four goals in a man-of-the-match performance. Playing at {{rlp|ce}}, Langi made his NRL debut for the Roosters on 19 August 2013, in their round 23 match against the [[Wests Tigers]]. On 27 August 2013, Langi was named at {{rlp|fe}} in the 2013 NYC Team of the Year. In 2016, Langi joined the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]]' feeder team, the [[North Sydney Bears]], in the NSW Cup.

===Penrith Panthers===
Langi joined the [[Penrith Panthers]] in 2017, playing for their [[NSW Cup]] team.

===Leigh Centurions===
In June 2017 he moved to [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] on a deal lasting until the end of the season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/40380400|title=Samisoni Langi: Leigh sign Tonga international until the end of the season|publisher=BBC Sport|date=23 June 2017|access-date=24 January 2018}}</ref>

===Catalans Dragons===
In November 2017, Langi signed with the [[Catalans Dragons]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/41852981|title=Samisoni Langi: Catalans Dragons sign Tonga international on two-year deal|publisher=BBC Sport|date=2 November 2017|access-date=24 January 2018}}</ref>
Langi played in the [[2018 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2018/aug/25/catalans-dragons-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history|work=The Guardian|date=25 August 2018|access-date=5 November 2019}}</ref>
On 9 October 2021, Langi played for [[Catalans Dragons]] in their [[2021 Super League Grand Final]] defeat against St. Helens.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58858850|title=Super League Grand Final: Catalans Dragons 10-12 St Helens: Saints win three in a row|publisher=BBC Sport}}</ref>

In the 2022 Super League season, Langi made 19 appearances including the clubs upset loss to Leeds in the elimination play off.

===Wakefield Trinity===
Langi then signed for [[Wakefield Trinity]] ahead of the 2023 Super League season and made his club debut in round 1 against his former club Catalans which saw Wakefield Trinity lose 24–38.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/64668377|title=Super League: Wakefield Trinity 24-38 Catalans Dragons – Tom Johnstone scores debut hat-trick against old club|publisher=BBC Sport}}</ref>

Langi played 14 games for Wakefield Trinity in the [[Super League XXVIII]] season as the club finished bottom of the table and were relegated to the [[RFL Championship]] which ended their 24-year stay in the top flight.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/20876/12962152/super-league-wakefield-trinity-relegated-after-loss-at-leigh-leopards-st-helens-and-wigan-warriors-win/|title=Super League: Wakefield Trinity relegated after loss at Leigh Leopards, St Helens and Wigan Warriors win|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>

On 3 November 2023 it was reported that he had left [[Wakefield Trinity]] after completing just one year of his two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=3 Nov 2023|accessdate=22 Dec 2023|title=Veteran Tonga and France international cuts short time at Wakefield Trinity following club’s relegation|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/veteran-tonga-and-france-international-cuts-short-time-at-wakefield-trinity-following-clubs-relegation}}</ref>

==International career==
===Tonga===
Langi was a member of Tonga's squad for the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup|2013 World Cup]]. He played in all three of Tonga's matches, kicking a total of 7 goals. On 19 October 2014, Langi played at {{rlp|fe}} in Tonga's match against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]].<ref>{{cite web|last=Lahoc|first=Gabriel|date=20 October 2014|title=Kumulswin|url=http://www.thenational.com.pg/kumulswin/|publisher=The National|access-date=7 May 2017}}</ref> On 2 May 2015, he played for Tonga in the 2015 [[Polynesian Cup]] against [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]]. He missed Tonga's first two conversion attempts, and the goal-kicking duties were given to [[Solomone Kata]] for the remainder of the match.

On 7 May 2016, Langi played for Tonga against Samoa in the 2016 [[Polynesian Cup]], suffering a game-ending injury inside the opening 10 minutes.<ref>{{cite web|last=Gabor|first=Martin|date=7 May 2016|title=Samoa holds on in pulsating Pacific Test|url=http://www.nrl.com/samoa-holds-on-in-pulsating-pacific-test/tabid/10874/newsid/95681/default.aspx|publisher=National Rugby League}}</ref>
Langi was named as 18th man for Tonga's [[2017 Pacific Rugby League Tests|2017 Pacific Test]] against [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]].<ref>{{cite web|date=28 April 2017|title=Samoa and Tonga squads announced|url=http://www.nrl.com/samoa-and-tonga-squads-announced/tabid/10874/newsid/106195/default.aspx|publisher=National Rugby League|access-date=28 April 2017}}</ref>

===France===
In 2022, Langi switched his allegiance from [[Tongan national rugby league team|Tonga]] where he represented them on 7 occasions, to play for [[French national rugby league team|France]] at this year's [[2021 Rugby League World Cup|Rugby league World Cup]], qualifying through residency. Langi made his debut for France in the opening round of the [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]] against {{rlnt|Greece}}.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63293849|title=Rugby League World Cup: France 34-12 Greece – Les Bleus beat debutants|publisher=BBC Sport}}</ref>
